title: Image 1 of Rutland weekly herald (Rutland, Vt.), April 2, 1863
description: RUTLAND HERALD V THURSDAY APRIL 2 18G3 Tbe Conscription law nod Poor Men A certain class of newspapers which occa sionally utter a loud call upon every man of every party to obey the laws whatever they be as if any but their own followers ever pro posed resistance to them are engaged at the saule time and all the time in misrepresenting the most important and necessary ones called ovietanfA hv the national struggle for IUIU j life and in fomenting disaffection thereto What can be the object of this constant labor except to stir up the very resistance to the laws which they dare not openly advocate Such is apparently enough its only tendency The conscription act of the last Congress is a law of the land If these journals are sincere in their call upon all to obey the laws why constantly misrepresent the object and effect of its provisions 1 Conscript laws of some kind have been a necessity in every great war Butprobably no law of the kind was tver framed which was so just and humane in its treatment of all classes bf the people or which so well protected the poor citizen We
